User.title: User
User.description: Represents a registered user of the FlowPro application. Authentication details are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and communication.

name.description: The user's full name or display name.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile was last updated.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

isOnboarded.description: Indicates if the user has completed the initial onboarding quiz.

QuizResponse.title: QuizResponse
QuizResponse.description: Stores a user's answers to the personalized sales profile quiz, used for generating action plans.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuizResponse entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who completed the quiz. (Relationship: User 1:N QuizResponse)

responses.description: An array of objects, each representing a question and its answer from the quiz.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the quiz was completed.

PersonalizedPlan.title: PersonalizedPlan
PersonalizedPlan.description: Holds the AI-generated sales action plan tailored for a specific user based on their quiz responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PersonalizedPlan entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User for whom this plan was generated. (Relationship: User 1:1 PersonalizedPlan)

quizResponseId.description: Reference to the QuizResponse that informed this personalized plan. (Relationship: QuizResponse 1:1 PersonalizedPlan)

strategy.description: The best strategy suggested for the user to achieve sales goals.

suggestedIncomeType.description: The type of income or product/service suggested for the user.

executionMethod.description: Detailed guidance on how the user should execute their sales plan.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the personalized plan was generated.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Tracks a user's premium access status and subscription plan details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who holds this subscription. (Relationship: User 1:N Subscription)

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'active', 'cancelled', 'expired').

startDate.description: The date when the subscription started.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is expected to end (null for lifetime plans).

paymentProviderSubscriptionId.description: Optional: Reference ID from the external payment provider for this subscription.

Mission.title: Mission
Mission.description: Defines a single day's or stage's objectives and the overall structure within the guided sales journey.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Mission entity.

title.description: The title of the mission (e.g., 'Dia 1', 'Dia 2', 'Primeira Venda').

order.description: The sequential order of the mission in the overall journey.

description.description: A brief description of the mission's goal or content.

MissionStep.title: MissionStep
MissionStep.description: Represents an individual task or sub-objective within a larger mission, providing practical instructions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MissionStep entity.

missionId.description: Reference to the Mission this step belongs to. (Relationship: Mission 1:N MissionStep)

order.description: The sequential order of this step within its mission.

title.description: A short title for the mission step (e.g., 'O que vender', 'Onde encontrar clientes').

instructions.description: A list of simple, practical instructions for completing this step.

suggestedProductService.description: A suggested product or service for the user to consider selling.

suggestedPrice.description: A suggested price point for the product or service.

suggestedSalesScriptId.description: Reference to a Script entity providing the main message for this step. (Relationship: Script 1:N MissionStep)

clientAcquisitionMethods.description: A list of places or methods to find clients (e.g., 'Instagram', 'empresas locais').

practicalStrategies.description: A list of practical strategies related to finding clients or making sales.

closingScriptIds.description: References to Script entities containing closing scripts relevant to this step. (Relationship: Script N:N MissionStep)

objectionResponses.description: A list of common objections and suggested responses (e.g., 'tá caro').

conversionStrategy.description: The recommended strategy for converting leads into sales at this stage.

callToAction.description: A clear, actionable instruction for the user to perform to complete the step.

UserMissionProgress.title: UserMissionProgress
UserMissionProgress.description: Records a user's progression through the guided missions and tracks their performance and simulated earnings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserMissionProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N UserMissionProgress)

missionId.description: Reference to the Mission this progress entry is for. (Relationship: Mission 1:N UserMissionProgress)

isCompleted.description: Indicates if the mission has been fully completed by the user.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the mission was completed.

currentMissionStepId.description: Reference to the MissionStep the user is currently on within this mission. (Relationship: MissionStep 1:N UserMissionProgress)

estimatedEarnings.description: A simulated estimate of earnings based on completed tasks or simulated sales.

lastActivityAt.description: Timestamp of the last interaction or progress update for this mission.

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a single conversational thread between a user and the AI Sales Mentor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this chat session. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatSession)

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session began.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session last had a message added.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Stores individual messages exchanged within an AI Sales Mentor chat session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatSessionId.description: Reference to the ChatSession this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatSession 1:N ChatMessage)

sender.description: The sender of the message ('user' or 'ai').

content.description: The text content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

Script.title: Script
Script.description: Provides pre-written sales, follow-up, or closing scripts for users to utilize in their interactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Script entity.

title.description: A descriptive title for the script (e.g., 'Initial Sales Outreach', 'Follow-up Email').

content.description: The actual text content of the sales script.

description.description: A brief explanation of when and how to use this script.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the script was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the script was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile data and serves as the root for all user-specific data. Security relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores a user's answers to the initial personalization quiz. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who completed the quiz.

params.name: quizResponseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific set of quiz responses.

definition.description: Stores the AI-generated personalized sales action plan for a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this plan belongs.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific personalized plan.

definition.description: Tracks a user's subscription status and plan details. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Critical for controlling access to premium features.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user holding the subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user's subscription.

definition.description: Defines the global structure and content of each mission (e.g., 'Dia 1', 'Dia 2'). These are read-only for subscribed users.

params.name: missionId
params.description: The unique identifier of a mission.

definition.description: Details the individual steps or tasks within a mission. These are read-only for subscribed users.

params.name: missionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent mission.

params.name: stepId
params.description: The unique identifier of a specific mission step.

definition.description: Records a specific user's progress through a mission, including completion status and estimated earnings. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose progress is being tracked.

params.name: progressId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user's mission progress entry.

definition.description: Represents an individual chat conversation session between a user and the AI Sales Mentor. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this chat session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chat session.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a user's chat session. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, crucial for direct security checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent chat session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent chat session.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific message within the chat session.

definition.description: Contains pre-written sales, follow-up, and closing scripts. These are global, read-only resources available to subscribed users.

params.name: scriptId
params.description: The unique identifier of a sales script.

firestore.reasoning:
The FlowPro Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This design criticaly avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies that would require `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document attributes. For all user-specific subcollections like `quizResponses`, `personalizedPlans`, `subscriptions`, `missionProgress`, `chatSessions`, and `chatMessages`, the `userId` is explicitly included in the document schema. This denormalization ensures that authorization rules for these documents can be evaluated solely based on `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`, making them atomic, efficient, and robust against race conditions or complex transaction scenarios. For instance, a `ChatMessage` document, despite being nested under a `ChatSession` (which is under a `User`), directly contains `userId`, enabling a direct `request.auth.uid` check without needing to read the parent `ChatSession` or `User` document. 

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture & QAPs:**

1.  **User-Specific Private Data:** All data directly owned by or specific to a single user is stored under a dedicated subcollection of their `/users/{userId}` document. This includes `/users/{userId}/quizResponses`, `/users/{userId}/personalizedPlans`, `/users/{userId}/subscriptions`, `/users/{userId}/missionProgress`, and `/users/{userId}/chatSessions`. This approach ensures **Query Authorization Predictability (QAPs)** because `list` operations (e.g., `db.collection('users').doc(userId).collection('quizResponses').get()`) inherently return only the data the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) is authorized to see. The security rules for these paths will simply check `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path wildcard or `resource.data.userId` from the document's denormalized field. This makes rules simple, explicit, and easy to debug.

2.  **Global Read-Only Content (Conditional Access):** Core application content like `missions`, `missionSteps`, and `scripts` are placed in top-level collections (`/missions/{missionId}`, `/scripts/{scriptId}`). These documents have a homogeneous security posture: they are read-only for *all authenticated users with an active subscription*. Access to these collections will be granted through a security rule that checks the user's subscription status, typically by performing a `get()` operation on a fixed path: `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}`. While this involves a `get()` call, it's considered an acceptable pattern for DBAC (Database Access Control) as it's a fixed-path lookup on the current user's *own* data, not a hierarchical dependency on parent content or an arbitrary read. This design avoids duplicating static content for each user, promoting efficiency and consistency. QAPs are maintained because any user querying `/missions` or `/scripts` will retrieve all available content, and the security rules will filter access based on their global subscription status.

**Summary:**
This structure ensures clear separation of concerns, with private user data residing in path-secured subcollections and global content residing in top-level collections with conditional access based on the user's subscription status. Denormalized `userId` fields enhance authorization independence, and the chosen path structures inherently support predictable query results, simplifying rule logic and overall system debugging.
